task_updates.executor_notes: Re-verified the staging rollout state before attempting the backfill observation. The linked project still reports `v20260424000000=false` and `v20260424000001=false`; `to_regclass('public.asset_comments')` and `to_regclass('public.media_import_jobs')` both return `NULL`; `to_regprocedure('internal.discord_promote_resources()')` and `to_regprocedure('internal.discord_promote_resources(boolean)')` both return `NULL`; and `cron.job` still has `promoter_job=false` and `importer_job=false`. Because the importer cron is not present, there was no runnable backfill to observe for 1-2 cycles, so no `media_import_jobs` drain data, per-channel asset sanity checks, Cloudflare quota validation, or storage-growth validation could be performed. Full-suite regression verification was still rerun as required: `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` finished with the same 7 existing failures already seen in prior batches and no new Discord-sync-specific failures surfaced.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: No. Re-verification still shows no deployed Discord-sync schema or cron state on staging: the relevant migration versions are unapplied, `asset_comments` and `media_import_jobs` are absent, the promoter functions are absent, and both Discord cron jobs are missing. Without an importer cron to run, no job-drain observation, end-to-end per-channel sanity check, Cloudflare quota review, or storage-growth validation was possible.
